interactions.summary: The interviewer asks what teacher collaboration looks like; Jiang describes mentoring, shared department planning, peer observation, and frank feedback, then distinguishes strong Chinese teaching culture from a test-score end goal that remains problematic.

Asked about alternative measures, Jiang says he prioritizes growth mindset, self-reflection, awareness of limitations, and courage, and argues that top academic students can become more rigid than weaker students.

claims.claim: Chinese teacher collaboration includes mentoring, collaborative lesson planning, peer classroom observation, and frank post-lesson critique.
Jiang distinguishes strong Chinese teaching culture from a problematic assessment system that still overvalues test scores and pressures students.
He wants holistic assessment that encourages individuality and creativity rather than only test performance.
He says growth mindset means valuing challenge, self-reflection, awareness of limitations, and courage to overcome those limitations.
Jiang argues that top academic students may be harder to teach than weak students because praise and easy rewards can make them closed-minded, stubborn, and anti-creative.

signature_moments.moment: The professional teaching culture is great; the end goal is the problem.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the culture of teaching... is great but the end goal... is problematic
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It prevents a simple China-bad reading by separating teacher culture from the assessment regime.

signature_moments.moment: The worst students may be more teachable than the best students because they know their limitations.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the very best students... are much more problematic than the worst students
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It sharpens his critique of meritocratic success as a deforming incentive.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Assessment that encourages individuality, creativity, growth mindset, and self-reflection rather than test scores alone.
A value system of challenge, self-reflection, awareness of limitations, and courage to overcome those limitations.

chronology_notes.note: On 2026-04-05 Jiang pairs praise for Chinese teacher culture with a sharper critique of test-score ends and meritocratic best-student values.
